title: Don Quixote
author: Cervantes Saavedra, Miguel de
summary: Don Quixote follows an aging hidalgo of La Mancha who, inspired by chivalric romances, becomes a self-styled knight-errant. Accompanied by his pragmatic squire Sancho Panza, he undertakes a series of comic and poignant adventures—battling windmills, freeing captives, misreading inns for castles, and seeking to disenchant his idealized lady Dulcinea del Toboso. The tale exposes books, honor, and social customs through pastiche, parody, and episodic encounters in the Sierra Morena, castles, courts and villages. Priests, barbers, nobles, shepherds, dukes and duchesses alternately mock, aid and manipulate the pair, testing courage, folly and the boundaries between fiction and reality, identity, language, society.

characters.name: Don Quixote (the ingenious gentleman of La Mancha)
characters.personality: idealistic, deluded, relentless in chivalric devotion
characters.lifespan: Undertakes many adventures; repeatedly tested, at times carried off 'enchanted' (see Ch. XLVII); end of fate not shown in provided excerpt

characters.name: Sancho Panza
characters.lifespan: Accompanies Don Quixote on many sallys; later briefly governs an 'island' (Ch. XLIV–XLVII) per TOC entries

characters.name: Dulcinea del Toboso (Aldonza Lorenzo, implied)
characters.personality: idealized, unattainable lady of Don Quixote's imagination
characters.lifespan: Object of Don Quixote's quests; subject of disenchantment schemes (Vol. II Ch. XXXIV–XXXV)

characters.name: The Curate (the parish priest)
characters.personality: practical, censorious, concerned with books and sanity
characters.lifespan: Inspects library and conspires to cure Don Quixote (Ch. VI; Vol. II Ch. I)

characters.name: The Barber
characters.personality: practical, conspiratorial, comic accomplice to the curate
characters.lifespan: Helps censor books and enact schemes; takes part in Sierra devices (Ch. VI, XXVII–XXVIII)

characters.name: Samson Carrasco (Bachelor)
characters.personality: learned, energetic, comic foil, later disguised as a knight
characters.lifespan: Debates Don Quixote and later confronts him as the Knight of the Mirrors (Vol. II Ch. III–V, XII–XV)

characters.name: Teresa Panza
characters.lifespan: Appears in domestic scenes; receives Sancho's letters and is involved in his governance (Vol. II Ch. V, XLIX)

characters.name: The Canon
characters.personality: learned critic of chivalric books, satirical voice
characters.lifespan: Pursues the subject of books and debates Don Quixote (Ch. XLVIII–L)

characters.name: The Duke and Duchess
characters.personality: amusingly cruel patrons who toy with Don Quixote and Sancho
characters.lifespan: Host elaborate deceptions for sport, including duchess's duenna and enchantments (Vol. II Ch. XXXIII–XLVIII)

characters.name: Doña Rodriguez (the Duchess's duenna)
characters.personality: serves her mistress in courtly intrigues
characters.lifespan: Involved in ducal entertainments and plots (Vol. II Ch. XLVIII–L)

characters.name: Princess Micomicona
characters.personality: fabricated distressed royal used to send Quixote on an adventure
characters.lifespan: Central to a contrived episode at an inn (Ch. XXXVII)

characters.name: Marcela (the shepherdess)
characters.personality: independent, pastoral, attracts attention and verses
characters.lifespan: Her story and the dead shepherd’s verses are episodes in book (Ch. XIII–XIV)

characters.name: The Dead Shepherd (Grisóstomo-like figure)
characters.lifespan: His despairing verses appear in Ch. XIV

characters.name: The Goatherds / Goatherd (singular)
characters.lifespan: Appear in Sierra and other pastoral episodes (Ch. XI–XII, LI–LII)

characters.name: Mambrino (name associated with helmet episode)
characters.lifespan: His 'helmet' (actually a barber's basin) becomes a prize for Don Quixote (Ch. XXI, XLV)

characters.name: Beltenebros (name used in penance imitation)
characters.personality: famous fictional penitent invoked by Quixote
characters.lifespan: Don Quixote imitates penance of Beltenebros in Sierra Morena (Ch. XXV)

characters.name: Maritornes
characters.age: inn servant (not named in excerpt but common character)
characters.personality: comic inn character (not explicit in given TOC)
characters.lifespan: Not specified in provided excerpt

characters.name: The Captive
characters.lifespan: Relates an extended life-and-adventure narrative while among Don Quixote's party (Ch. XXXIX–XLI)

characters.name: The Muleteer
characters.lifespan: Tells a memorable story at the inn (Ch. XLIII)

characters.name: The Officers of the Holy Brotherhood
characters.lifespan: Appear in the adventure of Chapter XLVI

characters.name: The Penitents
characters.lifespan: Their rare adventure is recounted (Ch. LII)

characters.name: Master Pedro (puppet-showman)
characters.lifespan: Performs with his ape; features in braying and puppet episodes (Vol. II Ch. XXV–XXVII)

characters.name: The Divining Ape (and its owner)
characters.lifespan: Performs divinations as an entertainment (Vol. II Ch. XXV–XXVII)

characters.name: Benengeli (the putative 'narrator' name)
characters.lifespan: Named as source/translator in an authorial episode (Vol. II Ch. XXVIII)

characters.name: Clavileño (the enchanted wooden horse)
characters.lifespan: Used in a protracted feigned adventure (Vol. II Ch. XLI)

characters.name: Camacho
characters.personality: wealthy groom in a comic wedding episode
characters.lifespan: Figure in the wedding of Camacho and related incidents (Vol. II Ch. XX–XXI)

characters.name: Basilio
characters.lifespan: His episode at Camacho's wedding is recounted (Vol. II Ch. XX)

characters.name: Altisidora
characters.lifespan: Attempts to woo Don Quixote during ducal entertainments (Vol. II Ch. XLVI)

characters.name: Doña Rodrí guez (the Countess/Duchess's duenna / the distressed duenna/Countess Trifaldi)
characters.personality: involved in ducal pranks and the 'distressed duenna' tale
characters.lifespan: Central to the Countess Trifaldi episode and its continuation (Vol. II Ch. XXXVI–XXXIX)

locations.name: El Toboso (Dulcinea's village, Toledo province, Spain)
Cueva de Montesinos (Montesinos cave, La Mancha region)
An inn (frequent episode settings, generic La Mancha inns)
